IBM BobBee
AI-native sales execution for Select Territory sellers
IBMer watsonx Challenge 2026
Path 1 · Built during the challenge
Business area — Sales

A seller owns 1,900 accounts. BobBee tells them the three that matter today — and drafts the outreach.

It's 7:40 AM. Tim Zhou opens BobBee. There's no dashboard to parse — just a sentence talking to him: "Good morning. Two accounts hit renewal risk overnight and one raised a round. I've reordered your day and drafted everything. Start here." Below it, the day's work is ranked, reasoned, and one click away.
How BobBee works, in one picture
Private signals
IBM Sales Cloud & install base · spend and relationship history · client decision-makers
+
Public signals
Company news · funding rounds · leadership changes · competitive moves
→
BobBee
Scores every account, sorts them into cadences, and drafts each touch
→
✓Ranked daily plan — who to work, in order
✓Drafted emails — in the seller's voice
✓Pre-call briefs — context before you dial
✓Morning brief — the day in one paragraph
~1,900
accounts per Select Territory book — only dozens are touchable each week
3 tools
ISC, ZoomInfo and Salesloft cross-referenced by hand to decide who to contact
~4 hrs
of daily triage and drafting before a single email is actually sent
The solution. BobBee turns a raw territory list into a ranked, cadenced, day-by-day plan of attack — who to email and call each day of the quarter, and why — then drafts every touch in the seller's voice. It doesn't report the day; it directs it.

IBM BobBee
How it works
A deterministic engine ranks the work.
watsonx.ai writes it.
Inside BobBee — from 1,900 accounts to today's three moves

Explainable ranking first. Generative drafting second.

Every ranking is inspectable — the seller can always see why an account landed where it did. watsonx sits on top of the pipeline, never underneath it.

Ask BobBee
watsonx Assistant Q&A
Granite (ibm/granite-4-h-small) on watsonx.ai powers the generative layer · a deterministic fallback keeps every surface working offline.
The seller always knows what is fact and what is suggestion
Blue means deterministic data
Rankings, scores, cadence positions and phone numbers — facts the pipeline computed and can explain.
Purple means watsonx-generated
Briefs, drafts and suggestions are marked, so the seller separates fact from model output at a glance — trust by design.
What the seller actually receives
Email draft — Ridgeline Pharma
Enterprise Expansion · Intro email · watsonx
Hi Dana, I partner with teams like yours on IBM Cloud Paks. The short version of why I'm writing: I think we can help you move workloads off a competing platform onto IBM. I also saw that a competitor contract is up for renewal. Open to a short conversation?
Pre-call brief — Westbridge Assurance
Whitespace Nurture · Second attempt · watsonx
Primary contact: Fatima Rivera, VP of Infrastructure. Relationship active; spend flat year over year. Likely objection: existing vendor lock-in. Open with the migration-cost angle and reference their recent cloud RFP.
Explainable pipeline · governed generative AIPage 2 of 3
IBM BobBee
Impact, differentiation and roadmap
About 3.25 hours reclaimed
per seller, every day
Productivity impact

Four hours of planning becomes forty-five minutes of doing.

Before BobBee — manual triage and drafting~4.0 hrs
With BobBee — read brief, review, send~0.75 hrs
Per day, per seller: about 5 minutes reading the ranked brief, 25 minutes reviewing pre-drafted emails, and 15 minutes on pre-call briefs. Frequency: Daily.
~16 hrs
reclaimed per seller every week — redirected from planning overhead into pipeline-moving work
Why it's different
Action before analytics
Most tools show the seller everything. BobBee tells them the one thing to do next, and moves the charts out of the way.
Provenance you can trust
A simple blue and purple convention makes what the system knows and what the model suggests visible at a glance.
Deterministic and generative
Explainable scoring does the ranking; watsonx writes the copy. Neither pretends to be the other.
One focused flow
Draft, send, next. Brief, dial, log, next. No context-switching across three source systems.
Roadmap
Next
Live watsonx grounding
Real ISC and install-base data feeding every brief and draft.
Then
Command-bar layer
"Log voicemail, retry Thursday" — keyboard-first, from anywhere.
Vision
Proactive plays
The system clusters the book into opportunities before the seller asks.
